formations_blocks: Qatar playing long down the line / direct territory-based approach
Qatar building out from the back at times
Bosnia and Herzegovina pressing and quickly recovering possession up the pitch
key_matchups: Sunjic recovering to stop Afif on the break
phases.summary: Bosnia and Herzegovina start with greater intent, pressing higher and creating the clearer early chances, with Demirovic and Sunjic both forcing Abunada into saves. Qatar alternate between direct clearances and some buildup from the back, but their best moments come in transition and on the break, where Afif briefly threatens before Sunjic recovers to snuff out the danger.

adjustments: Hydration break interrupts play and both sides restart after the pause
Bosnia and Herzegovina repeatedly target the left side for a free-kick delivery and attacking dribble entry
Qatar respond after conceding twice and manage a quick reduction of the deficit before half-time
key_matchups: Alajbegovic vs Qatar’s defensive line on the dribble and shot at 29'
Katic vs the far-post defensive coverage from Bosnia's right-sided free-kick at 28'
Alajbegovic’s free-kick hits Khouki in the wall at 23'
phases.summary: The phase is punctuated by a hydration break, but Bosnia and Herzegovina gradually turn a relatively quiet spell into a decisive attacking burst. Alajbegovic drives the breakthrough with a solo goal at 29', Bosnia double their lead at 34', and then Qatar react with a goal of their own at 42' to keep the contest alive before the interval.

The commentary only confirms the interval score, with Bosnia and Herzegovina leading Qatar 2-1 at half-time. No tactical structure, pressing block, adjustments, or specific matchups are described in this phase.

formations_blocks: Qatar spending time in a more reactive defensive phase with intermittent time in the final third
Bosnia and Herzegovina pressing/attacking through wide areas rather than sustained penetration
adjustments: Bosnia and Herzegovina: Memic for Malic and Tahirovic for Sunjic at 47'
Qatar: Al Ganehi replaces the injured Al Haydos at 56'
Bosnia and Herzegovina: Mahmic and Hadžikadunić on for Katic and Dzeko at 64'
Bosnia and Herzegovina planning a change as the match progresses
key_matchups: Afif vs Bosnia and Herzegovina back line in the box
Al Ganehi driving from the left vs Bosnia and Herzegovina defenders
Dzeko vs Qatar’s defensive line/crosses into the box
Vasilj vs Katic in a collision for a high ball
phases.summary: Bosnia and Herzegovina controlled possession for much of the phase, but their territorial dominance was fairly slow-moving and largely built through crosses and balls over the top. Qatar threatened sporadically, especially through Afif and the newly introduced Al Ganehi on the left, while both sides made substitutions that altered the rhythm more than the overall structure. Bosnia’s late changes, including the removal of Dzeko, suggest a tactical reshuffle as the game entered a more managed and cautious stage.

formations_blocks: Bosnia and Herzegovina slowing the game down in possession
Qatar with the majority of the ball late on
Bosnia and Herzegovina effectively managing the closing stages rather than pressing high
adjustments: Fathy booked and then substituted off
Bosnia and Herzegovina allowed to slow things down after the 78' booking and subs
Six minutes of stoppage time added
key_matchups: Bajraktarevic vs Qatar's goalkeeper (80' shot saved/palmed away, then goal follows from the same attack)
Miguel vs Qatar's goalkeeper (90+4' long-range shot tipped over)
Alajbegovic vs Fathy (Fathy roughing him up, leading to a booking)
phases.summary: Bosnia and Herzegovina managed the closing phase intelligently, slowing the tempo after the hour-mark and then capitalising when Bajraktarevic’s right-side cutting runs began to create danger. Qatar had more of the ball late on, but the match was already slipping away from them after Bosnia’s 80th-minute goal made it 3-1 and effectively settled the contest.
